title: Daily Briefing — Monday, May 25
tldr: DeepSeek's 75% price cut on V4-Pro is now permanent — frontier-class reasoning at ~¼ the cost of OpenAI/Anthropic tokens.
Anthropic on track for its first quarterly operating profit; ~$10.9B projected Q2 revenue (+130% QoQ). OpenAI preparing a confidential S-1.
NextEra–Dominion $67B utility merger — largest in US history — explicitly to power AI workloads. Trump canceled a planned AI executive order.

blocks.p: The temporary 75% discount on V4-Pro is now permanent — roughly a quarter the cost of equivalent OpenAI/Anthropic tokens.
blocks.doIt: Re-run build-vs-buy and inference-cost math; an open-weight, ultra-cheap option changes the calculus for high-volume tasks.

blocks.p: Anthropic projects its first-ever quarterly operating profit (~$10.9B Q2 revenue, +130% QoQ). OpenAI is preparing a confidential S-1 (IPO path). Reported compute commitments run to ~$1.25B/month through 2029.
blocks.why: The leading labs are transitioning from cash-burning research shops to scaled businesses — and to public-market scrutiny.

blocks.p: NextEra Energy announced a $67B acquisition of Dominion Energy — the largest US utility merger ever — with AI-driven power demand as the stated rationale.
blocks.why: Electricity and grid capacity are now first-order constraints on AI scaling, not a footnote.

blocks.p: President Trump canceled the signing of a planned AI executive order, reportedly after direct lobbying from tech leaders who argued it risked America's competitive edge.
blocks.doIt: Don't bank on near-term US federal AI rules; plan around state laws (CA, CO) and the EU timeline instead.

sources: buildfastwithai (May 25 roundup) · The Information · Axios · crescendo.ai · agilebrandguide.com (several figures reported same-day; treat as directional).
